fault_hypothesis: The condition on line 77 is inverted: clamping is applied when `not clamp` is True (i.e., clamp=False), but it should be applied when clamp=True. The `if not clamp:` should be `if clamp:`.

constraints.intent: When clamp=True the branch body (clamping logic) should execute, but the inverted condition causes it to execute only when clamp=False
The then-branch (clamping) should only execute when clamp is False under current buggy logic, but should execute when clamp is True

spec.expr: (not clamp) or (y__4 >= min(y0, y1) and y__4 <= max(y0, y1))

constraints.intent: After the clamping block, if clamp=True then y should be within [min(y0,y1), max(y0,y1)]

spec.expr: clamp == (y__4 == max(min(y__1, max(y0, y1)), min(y0, y1)))

constraints.intent: When clamp=True, y__4 should equal the clamped value of y__1 to [min(y0,y1), max(y0,y1)]

spec.expr: (not clamp) or (__cbfl_result <= max(y0, y1) + 1e-9 and __cbfl_result >= min(y0, y1) - 1e-9)

constraints.intent: When clamp=True the returned value must be within the reference point range
